ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "driver_lic" TEXT /* Driver's license number */,
  "p_f_name" TEXT /* First name */,
  "p_l_name" TEXT /* Last name */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address */,
  "phone" TEXT /* Phone number */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth */,
  "occupation" TEXT /* Occupation */,
  "customer_type" TEXT /* Type of customer (e.g., policyholder, beneficiary, claimant) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "addresses" (
  "address_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each address */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer the address belongs to */,
  "street_addr" TEXT /* Street address */,
  "city" TEXT /* City */,
  "state" TEXT /* State */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country */,
  "address_status" TEXT /* Status of the address (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_addresses_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "address_types" (
  "address_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each address type */,
  "address_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the address type */,
  "address_type_description" TEXT /* Description of the address type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("address_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_addresses" (
  "customer_address_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer-address association */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "address_id" INTEGER /* ID of the address */,
  "address_type_id" INTEGER /* ID of the address type */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Effective date of the address */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Expiration date of the address */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_addresses_address_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_id") REFERENCES addresses ("address_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_addresses_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_addresses_address_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("address_type_id") REFERENCES address_types ("address_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "contact_numbers" (
  "contact_number_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each contact number */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer the contact number belongs to */,
  "contact_number" TEXT /* Contact number */,
  "contact_type" TEXT /* Type of contact number (e.g., home, work, mobile) */,
  "contact_status" TEXT /* Status of the contact number (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("contact_number_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_contact_numbers_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_contacts" (
  "customer_contact_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer-contact association */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "contact_number_id" INTEGER /* ID of the contact number */,
  "effective_date" TEXT /* Effective date of the contact number */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Expiration date of the contact number */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_contact_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_contacts_contact_number_id FOREIGN KEY ("contact_number_id") REFERENCES contact_numbers ("contact_number_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_contacts_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "policyholders" (
  "policyholder_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each policyholder */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "policy_number" TEXT /* Policy number */,
  "policy_type" TEXT /* Type of policy */,
  "policy_status" TEXT /* Status of the policy */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("policyholder_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_policyholders_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "beneficiaries" (
  "beneficiary_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each beneficiary */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "beneficiary_type" TEXT /* Type of beneficiary */,
  "beneficiary_relationship" TEXT /* Relationship of the beneficiary to the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("beneficiary_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_beneficiaries_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "claimants" (
  "claimant_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each claimant */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "claim_type" TEXT /* Type of claim */,
  "claim_status" TEXT /* Status of the claim */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("claimant_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_claimants_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "audits" (
  "audit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each audit log */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "audit_date" TEXT /* Date of the audit */,
  "audit_type" TEXT /* Type of audit (e.g., create, update, delete) */,
  "audit_description" TEXT /* Description of the audit */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("audit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_audits_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_controls" (
  "access_control_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access control setting */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access (e.g., read-only, read-write) */,
  "access_status" TEXT /* Status of the access control setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_control_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_controls_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "integrations" (
  "integration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each integration setting */,
  "system_name" TEXT /* Name of the integrated system */,
  "integration_type" TEXT /* Type of integration (e.g., policy management, claims processing) */,
  "integration_status" TEXT /* Status of the integration setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("integration_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_integration" (
  "customer_integration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer-integration association */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "integration_id" INTEGER /* ID of the integration setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_integration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_integration_integration_id FOREIGN KEY ("integration_id") REFERENCES integrations ("integration_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_customer_integration_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
